prompt: A team member keeps missing deliverables, and training isn't the issue. Put the project manager's steps in order, from first to last.

steps.text: Hold a private 1:1 to discuss the performance and understand root causes
steps.note: Start supportively and in private — never raise it publicly.

steps.text: Follow up with regular check-ins and document expectations, examples and outcomes
steps.note: Build an objective record before any formal step.

steps.text: Agree a formal Performance Improvement Plan (PIP) with measurable goals and a timeline
steps.note: Formalise the support and a fair chance to improve.

steps.text: Escalate to the functional / resource manager and inform the sponsor of project impact
steps.note: Bring your documentation; personnel decisions often sit with the resource manager.

steps.text: Involve HR to manage reassignment or replacement, then update the plan and the team
steps.note: Replacement is the last resort — handled with HR and due process.

explanation: PMI's people focus is early, supportive, documented intervention before escalation: coach first, document, formalise a PIP, escalate with evidence, and only then involve HR for replacement — protecting both the individual and the project.
references: PMBOK Guide Seventh Edition — Team performance domain
